calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. HARLAN M. REESE, Esq. of the Reese Law Group (Tel: 760-842-5850) for Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Ford Motor Credit Company LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear.

The Court and plaintiff's counsel discuss plaintiff's counsel's efforts to confer with the defendant/judgment debtor Arlo H. Hemphill. Matter submitted.

The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Harlan M. Reese of the law offices of REESE LAW GROUP (760.842.5850) appears as counsel for opposing party, judgment creditor, plaintiff Ford Motor Company

No other appearance is made in this matter.

The Court hears from counsel for judgment creditor on record as reflected in the notes of the Certified Shorthand Reporter. It is determined that the levying office is holding zero funds as a result of this action. Plaintiff/creditor's counsel states funds are owed. Court directs counsel to contact judgment debtor and make an attempt to reach an agreement, payment plan. Plaintiff counsel agrees.

HEARING ON JUDGMENT CREDITOR'S OBJECTION TO JUDGEMENT DEBTOR'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is CONTINUED to November 18, 2020 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 302.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ARLO HEMPHILL MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT and/or in the alternative motion to stay execution of the writ of Garnishment. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, untimely service per CCP section 1005. Further, no competent evidence supporting motion.
